genre: Jazz/R&B (Americana Soul, blues, indie folk, rock)

location.origin: Spokane, Washington -- first artist in the engine from eastern Washington (east of the Cascades); moved to Seattle at age 20 in September 2007
location.formative: Pike Place Market, Seattle -- over eight years busking (2007-2015); Art Institute of Seattle (enrolled for audio engineering, quit after two quarters); self-trained through daily street performance; Rainier Valley residential connection; named best street performer in the Northwest (Seattle Weekly 2014)
location.career: Seattle-based 2007-2021; Rainier Valley residential; performed at Pike Place Market, The Triple Door, The Showbox, Neumos, The Crocodile, Dimitriou's Jazz Alley, Benaroya Hall (Seattle Symphony); European tour 2018; Newport Folk Festival with Black Opry 2023; relocated to Asheville NC 2022, then Nashville TN 2024 (Hurricane Helene displacement)

members.name: Whitney Monge
members.note: Solo artist; self-described 'Americana Soul'; Black woman; third woman in the engine (after Anderson degree 3, Bown degree 6); broke fourteen-degree gender gap (7-19); self-trained via busking at Pike Place Market

theory_nodes.concept: Sustained vocal projection in open space (the busker's sustain -- maintaining vocal tone, presence, and intensity in acoustically hostile environments; raw endurance without amplification or band support; eight years of daily outdoor performance developing projective vocal power with warmth; the voice as instrument of survival in public space)
examples: Whitney Monge -- Pike Place Market, voice sustaining over street noise, traffic, footsteps, conversation; the busker's vocal technique: powerful but not harsh, projected but not screamed, sustained but not monotonous; smoky soulful quality developed through outdoor exposure

Ernestine Anderson (degree 3) -- jazz vocal sustain in controlled environments (clubs, studios); lyrical sustain for musical reasons, extending melodic line; energy 2; controlled acoustics (comparison -- controlled vs. hostile environment)
Reggie Watts solo (degree 17) -- technological sustain via loop pedals creating sustained layers; electronic sustain vs. Monge's raw sustain (comparison -- electronic vs. acoustic)
Fly Moon Royale (degree 19) -- Parker's vocal sustain carrying collective identity across rotating ensembles; collective context vs. Monge's solo context (comparison -- supported vs. unsupported)
Maktub (degree 18) -- Watts's sustained vocal presence over fixed band groove; band support vs. solo acoustic (comparison)
theory_nodes.description: Sustained vocal projection -- the technique of maintaining vocal tone, presence, and emotional intensity through extended phrases in acoustically challenging environments -- is the primary technique at degree 20. This is not belting (pure volume), not melisma (ornamental movement), not vibrato (oscillation around a pitch). It is the ability to hold -- to sustain a note, a phrase, a presence -- against environmental resistance. Monge developed this technique through over eight years of daily outdoor performance at Pike Place Market, where the voice must carry without amplification over ambient noise. The busker's sustain is raw endurance: the performer's body producing and maintaining sound in open air, competing with the environment, sustaining through effort. This is the most exposed vocal technique in the engine -- every prior vocal artist performed in controlled environments with acoustic support. Vocal branch extends to ten nodes, tying keyboard/piano as the richest instrumental branch.

ancestors: Jazz vocal sustain -- Anderson (degree 3); lyrical sustain in controlled environment
Technological vocal sustain -- Watts solo (degree 17); loop pedals creating sustained layers
Band-supported vocal sustain -- Maktub (degree 18); vocal over fixed groove
Collective vocal sustain -- FMR (degree 19); vocal carrying identity across rotating ensemble
Blues vocal tradition -- national context for raw vocal projection in exposed settings
genealogy.relationship: Sustained vocal projection descends from the vocal theory branch (Anderson 3, Watts 17, Maktub 18, FMR 19) but represents the most exposed deployment context: solo acoustic without amplification, band support, or controlled acoustics. The busker's sustain is a survival skill -- Monge had no choice but to develop projective endurance. The five vocal entries now span five deployment contexts: solo jazz (3), solo performance-art (17), fixed band (18), rotating collective (19), solo acoustic busking (20). Vocal branch at ten nodes ties keyboard/piano as richest branch.

theory_nodes.concept: Guitar as one-person rhythm section (percussive bluesy acoustic guitar creating continuous sound through rhythmic repetition, open tunings, body percussion, overlapping fingerpicking; born of necessity -- the busker cannot bring a rhythm section to the street; the guitar provides melody, harmony, rhythm, and groove simultaneously)
examples: Whitney Monge -- percussive acoustic guitar, Pike Place Market; open tunings letting strings ring; fingerpicking patterns that overlap and create continuous sound; percussive hits on guitar body; one instrument as complete rhythm section

Bill Frisell (degree 1) -- guitar-as-texture with effects and looping; electronic sustain creating soundscapes (comparison -- opposite pole of guitar practice: processed vs. raw, electronic vs. acoustic)
Overton Berry (degree 5) -- piano as one-person groove source; self-contained instrument providing rhythm and harmony (comparison -- different instrument, same one-person-rhythm-section principle)
Maktub (degree 18) -- guitar as part of four-piece ensemble; Goldman's guitar within collective arrangement (comparison -- guitar as ensemble voice vs. guitar as solo rhythm section)
theory_nodes.description: The guitar-as-rhythm-section concept describes the solo acoustic guitarist's technique of making one instrument provide all the functions of a full rhythm section: melody (vocal line supported by guitar figures), harmony (chord voicings and suspensions), rhythm (percussive strumming and body hits), and groove (repetitive patterns creating continuous rhythmic feel). This technique was developed by Monge out of necessity -- a busker at Pike Place Market cannot bring a drummer, bassist, and keyboardist to the street. The result is a percussive, bluesy guitar style that creates sustain through rhythmic repetition rather than electronic effects or acoustic resonance. Open tunings let strings ring sympathetically; fingerpicking patterns overlap to create continuous sound; hits on the guitar body add rhythmic texture. This forms the opposite pole from Frisell's guitar-as-texture (degree 1): where Frisell uses effects and looping to create sustained electronic soundscapes, Monge uses physical technique and tuning to create sustained acoustic rhythm.

ancestors: Guitar-as-texture -- Frisell (degree 1); effects and looping creating sustained soundscapes (opposite pole)
Piano as groove source -- Berry (degree 5); one instrument as self-contained rhythm section
Blues acoustic guitar tradition -- national context for percussive solo guitar technique
Busking tradition -- street performance forcing self-sufficiency in instrumental accompaniment
genealogy.relationship: The guitar-as-rhythm-section extends the guitar theory thread from Frisell (degree 1) to Monge (degree 20) -- nineteen degrees apart, opposite poles of guitar practice. Frisell: processed, electronic, textural, ambient. Monge: raw, acoustic, percussive, rhythmic. The self-accompaniment principle connects to Berry (degree 5) piano as self-contained groove instrument. Both Berry and Monge are self-taught artists who developed their one-person-rhythm-section technique through sustained public performance.

theory_nodes.concept: Jazz-as-method continuing (third consecutive Jazz-as-modifier degree -- 18, 19, 20; Jazz/R&B genre label where jazz harmony underlies Americana Soul compositions; orchestral arrangements by Andrew Joslyn reveal extended voicings and modal ambiguity beneath folk-soul surface; genre evolution stage four continues: modifier-inversion stable across three degrees)
examples: Whitney Monge -- Seattle Symphony orchestrations by Andrew Joslyn revealing jazz-informed harmonic structure beneath folk-soul surface; what sounds like simple chord progressions in solo guitar context opens up to extended voicings and modal ambiguity when orchestrated
Maktub (degree 18) -- Jazz/Soul label; jazz harmony underlying neo-soul/funk composition; first jazz-as-modifier degree (comparison)
Fly Moon Royale (degree 19) -- Jazz/Soul label; jazz vocabulary as lingua franca for rotating collective; second jazz-as-modifier degree (comparison)
Jeff Lorber (degree 16) -- Jazz Fusion; jazz as primary genre in compound label; the last degree before modifier-inversion began (comparison -- compound vs. modifier)
theory_nodes.description: The Jazz/R&B genre label at degree 20 continues the jazz-as-modifier pattern established at degrees 18-19. Three consecutive degrees with Jazz as modifier confirms the pattern's stability: jazz is no longer the genre label but the compositional DNA -- the harmonic vocabulary underlying music whose primary identity is soul, R&B, Americana, folk. The genre evolution: single genre (Jazz, degrees 0-15) to compound (Jazz Fusion, 16) to superposition (Jazz/Comedy, 17) to modifier-inversion (Jazz/Soul 18-19, Jazz/R&B 20). Three degrees of modifier-inversion suggests this is not a transitional phase but a stable state. The Seattle Symphony orchestrations are the most revealing evidence: Monge's compositions, which sound like folk-soul in solo guitar context, open up to reveal jazz-informed extended voicings and modal ambiguity when orchestrated by Andrew Joslyn with a forty-piece orchestra.

ancestors: Jazz-as-modifier -- Maktub (degree 18); first instance of jazz as secondary genre label
Jazz-as-modifier -- FMR (degree 19); second instance, collective context
Genre evolution stages -- single (0-15), compound (16), superposition (17), modifier (18-20)
genealogy.relationship: Three consecutive jazz-as-modifier degrees (18, 19, 20) confirm the pattern's stability. The genre evolution has reached a plateau: jazz has completed its transformation from primary genre identity to background harmonic vocabulary. Each of the three modifier degrees applies jazz to a different primary genre: Soul (18, 19), R&B/Americana (20). The modifier is stable; the modified genre varies. This suggests degree 21 may either continue the modifier pattern or introduce a fifth genre evolution stage.

theory_nodes.concept: Blue note -- Americana-soul (twentieth deployment; vocal bending between minor and major thirds over solo acoustic guitar in blues/folk context; the most historically rooted deployment -- closest to the country-blues origin of the blue note technique; full circle from jazz blue note at degree 0 to blues blue note at degree 20; fifth vocal deployment)
examples: Whitney Monge (degree 20) -- solo vocal bending in blues/folk context over acoustic guitar; the blue note returning to its country-blues vocal origin; open-air acoustic deployment
Fly Moon Royale (degree 19) -- collective-soul blue note; syncopated vocal bending in rotating ensemble context (comparison -- collective vs. solo)
Ernestine Anderson (degree 3) -- jazz vocal blue note; Central District, energy 2 (comparison -- jazz vs. blues context; first vocal deployment)
Quincy Jones (degree 0) -- bass-line blue note; the engine's first deployment; jazz orchestral context (comparison -- the origin point vs. the return to blues origin)
theory_nodes.description: Twentieth deployment of the blue note thread. The Americana-soul blue note operates in the blues/folk tradition -- vocal bending between minor and major thirds over solo acoustic guitar. This is the most historically rooted deployment in the engine: the blue note originated in African American vocal traditions (field hollers, spirituals, country blues) as a micro-tonal bending between scale degrees. The engine's first deployment (Jones, degree 0) was in a jazz orchestral context -- the blue note as an element of sophisticated harmonic language. Monge's deployment at degree 20 returns the blue note to its vocal-acoustic origin: one voice, bending pitch expressively over simple guitar accompaniment. Full circle from degree 0 to degree 20. Fifth vocal deployment (Anderson 3, Watts solo 17, Maktub 18, FMR 19, Monge 20). Twenty mechanisms documented across twenty-one degrees.

ancestors: Blue note -- all nineteen prior deployments (degrees 0-19)
Blue note -- collective-soul (degree 19, FMR); immediately preceding vocal deployment
Blue note -- bass-line (degree 0, Jones); the engine's first deployment, jazz orchestral context
Country-blues vocal tradition -- the historical origin of the blue note technique; field hollers, spirituals, Delta blues
genealogy.relationship: Twenty deployments across twenty-one degrees. Fifth vocal deployment (Anderson 3, Watts solo 17, Maktub 18, FMR 19, Monge 20). Twenty mechanisms: bass-line (0), guitar (1), vocal (3), trumpet-warm (4), piano crush (5), altered voicings (6), trombone slide (7), bass fundamental (8), sax voice (9), alto cool bend (10), flute bent (11), bebop chromatic (12), modal integration (13), post-genre floating (14), assertive brass (15), synthesizer pitch bend (16), vocal glide (17), soul-vocal (18), collective-soul (19), americana-soul (20). The Americana-soul blue note is the most historically rooted: closest to the country-blues origin. Full circle from the jazz orchestral deployment at degree 0 to the blues vocal deployment at degree 20.

seattle_connection: Spokane native who moved to Seattle at age 20 (2007), quit the Art Institute after two quarters, and spent over eight years busking at Pike Place Market. Named best street performer in the Northwest (Seattle Weekly 2014). Self-described 'Americana Soul' -- fusion of soul, blues, indie folk, and rock. Black woman -- THIRD woman in the engine, breaking fourteen-degree gender gap (7-19). Rainier Valley residential (NEW neighborhood thread). Recorded at Studio Litho (Stone Gossard) and Robert Lang Studios. Sold-out Triple Door, Showbox, Neumos, Crocodile, Jazz Alley. Appeared in Find Your Way: A Busker's Documentary (2014). Performed with Seattle Symphony at Benaroya Hall (2021) -- twelve original songs orchestrated by Andrew Joslyn, conducted by Lee Mills. Black Fret / Sonic Guild grant recipient (2020). European tour 2018. Newport Folk Festival with Black Opry (2023). Left Seattle 2021, eventually Nashville via Asheville. Won 2025 Cayamo Soundcheck Contest. THE STREET LAUREATE pattern: twenty-first artist-city pattern. Non-institutional origin, public-space career, upward trajectory through city's institutions, eventual departure. SUSTAIN as twenty-first acoustic progression element. SIXTH consecutive energy-3 (15-20). Distribution 2:13:6. Raw-projective subtype. Jazz/R&B genre continues jazz-as-modifier (three consecutive, 18-20). DUWAMISH TERRITORIAL ACKNOWLEDGMENT implemented at degree 20 -- retroactive to all prior releases.

theory_genealogy: Sustained vocal projection in open space (the busker's sustain -- raw endurance without amplification, eight years of outdoor performance, the most exposed vocal technique in the engine; fifth vocal entry, vocal branch at ten nodes ties keyboard/piano as richest branch)

Guitar as one-person rhythm section (percussive acoustic guitar, open tunings, body percussion; born of necessity for solo busker; opposite pole from Frisell degree 1 guitar-as-texture with effects/looping; self-accompaniment principle connects to Berry degree 5 piano as self-contained groove instrument)

Jazz-as-method continuing (third consecutive Jazz-as-modifier degree, 18-19-20; Jazz/R&B label where jazz harmony underlies Americana Soul compositions; genre evolution stage four stable across three degrees; orchestral arrangements reveal jazz-informed structure beneath folk-soul surface)

Blue note -- twentieth deployment; Americana-soul (vocal bending in blues/folk context over solo acoustic guitar; most historically rooted deployment -- closest to country-blues origin; fifth vocal deployment; full circle from jazz orchestral at degree 0 to blues vocal at degree 20)
immediate_items: Genre rotation: TWENTY jazz-adjacent degrees (0-17 primary/compound, 18-20 Jazz as modifier). Jazz-as-method continues three consecutive. Track degree 21 genre label.
Jazz-as-method: THREE consecutive degrees (18, 19, 20) with Jazz as modifier. Pattern confirmed stable. Track continuation or disruption at degree 21.
Gender gap BROKEN at 20: third woman (Monge). Distribution 3:18. All three women are Black. All three eventually left Seattle. Track whether degree 21 continues gender diversity or returns to male.
Rainier Valley (NEW at 20): fifth neighborhood thread. First appearance. Track whether subsequent degrees return or single-degree introduction.
Sustain (NEW at 20): twenty-first acoustic progression element. Busker's raw vocal projection. Track progression.
The Street Laureate (NEW at 20): twenty-first artist-city pattern. Non-institutional origin, public-space career, upward trajectory, departure. Track.
Energy-3 cluster: SIX members (Marriott 15, Lorber 16, Watts 17, Maktub 18, FMR 19, Monge 20). SIX consecutive -- UNPRECEDENTED. Distribution 2:13:6. Track whether streak continues.
Solo artist returns at 20: organizational arc completes full circle -- solo (0-17) -> band (18) -> collective (19) -> solo (20). Track next organizational type.
Eastern Washington thread (NEW at 20): TWO artists (Lanphere 12 Wenatchee, Monge 20 Spokane). Track.
Self-taught thread EXTENDED: Berry (5) autodidact + Monge (20) busker-trained. Track.
Vocal branch EXTENDED to TEN nodes -- ties keyboard/piano as richest branch. Track.
Cornish pipeline: THREE artists (Priester 7, Caliman 9, Watts 17). Stable. Not applicable to Monge. Track.
Bebop genealogy: root (12) through americana-soul (20). Nine degrees wide. Track widening.
Loop pedal lineage: Frisell (1) to Watts solo (17). PAUSED at 18-19-20 (band/collective/solo-acoustic, not loop technology). Track resumption.
Band-as-collective thread: TWO entries (Maktub 18, FMR 19). Stable. Track.
Groove-as-composition: THREE entries (Berry 5, Maktub 18, FMR 19). Stable. Track.
Neo-soul thread: TWO entries (Maktub 18, FMR 19). Monge is adjacent (soul-influenced) but not neo-soul proper. Stable. Track.
Capitol Hill geography: TWO entries (Horvitz 2, Maktub 18). Monge performed there but is Rainier Valley-based. Track.
Multi-degree artist: ONE instance (Watts 17/18). Track.
Addiction-and-recovery thread: one instance (Lanphere 12). Track.
Women-departure thread (NEW at 20): all three women left Seattle (Anderson returned, Bown did not, Monge did not). Track.
Guitar thread bookends: Frisell (1) effects/texture to Monge (20) raw/percussion. Nineteen degrees. Track.
Duwamish territorial acknowledgment: RESOLVED at degree 20. Retroactive. All subsequent releases inherit.
Various source depth items carried from degrees 5-19.
pattern_items: Artist-city taxonomy: TWENTY-ONE named patterns (adding The Street Laureate at degree 20). Classify every degree.
Educator spectrum: eight modes. Monge is educator-through-presence (mentorship-by-visibility, advocacy for women and people of color). Track.

Composition-improvisation spectrum: SIXTEEN positions. Solo-acoustic-busker position added at degree 20 (improvisation in real-time response to street audience, spontaneous setlist adjustment). New dimension: audience-responsive improvisation.
Rhythmic spectrum: SIXTEEN positions. Percussive acoustic guitar rhythm (body hits, open tuning sustain) added at degree 20.
Chromaticism thread: TWENTY deployments. Americana chromatic added at degree 20 (chromatic passing tones in blues vocal bending and guitar fills serving folk/soul feel).

Blue note thread: TWENTY deployments across twenty-one degrees. Americana-soul at 20 -- fifth vocal deployment, most historically rooted (closest to country-blues origin). Twenty mechanisms documented.
Artist-as-institution spectrum: TWENTY-ONE positions. The Street Laureate added (non-institutional origin, public-space career).
Keyboard/piano theory branch: ten nodes. Stable. Now TIED with vocal as richest.
Vocal theory branch: TEN nodes. EXTENDED at 20 (sustained vocal projection). TIES keyboard/piano as richest branch. Five deployment contexts.
Bass theory branch: FIVE nodes. Stable.
Brass theory branch: four nodes. Stable.
Saxophone/reed theory branch: seven nodes. Stable.
Flute theory branch: two nodes. Stable.
Guitar theory branch: EXTENDED at 20 (guitar-as-rhythm-section, percussive acoustic). Bookends with Frisell (1).
Body-as-sound-source thread: TWELVE instruments. Extended (guitar body percussion at 20).

Amiga Principle gradient: SIXTEEN scales. Solo-busker Amiga Principle added at degree 20 (one person maximizing output through multi-instrument technique -- voice as lead, guitar as rhythm section, body as percussion).
Educational pipeline diversity: TWENTY-ONE pipelines. Extended (Art Institute dropout / street-trained busker pipeline).
Gender-barrier gradient: three women, eighteen men in twenty-one degrees. IMPROVED from 2:18 to 3:18. Fourteen-degree gap BROKEN. All three women are Black. All three eventually left Seattle.
Jackson Street corridor: Monge is not Jackson Street era. Different neighborhood (Rainier Valley). Track.
Label threads: all prior stable. Self-released thread EXTENDED (FMR 19 independent, Monge 20 self-released). Two entries.
Electronic jazz waves: four waves (from 16). Monge is acoustic/organic. Track.
Zen thread: from degree 8. Not applicable. Track.

Energy spectrum: 2:13:6. SIX consecutive energy-3 artists. UNPRECEDENTED streak. Six subtypes: acoustic-assertive (15), electronic-propulsive (16), performative-explosive (17), groove-collective (18), soul-communal (19), raw-projective (20).
Three pipelines at three members: Berklee, UW, Cornish. Stable. Track fourth.
West Coast / LA pipeline: three artists (8, 9, 10). Stable.
Within-family groups: vocal QUINTET (3/17/18/19/20 -- EXTENDED at 20), keyboard quintet (5/6/13/14/16). Vocal group now LARGEST, exceeding keyboard.
Central District oscillation: present (0-6), absent (7-10), returns (11), absent (12-18), returns (19), Rainier Valley (20). Track whether CD continues or Rainier Valley is the new thread.
Bebop genealogy tree: widening to include americana-soul (degree 20). Tree spans nine degrees (12-20).
Genre evolution: FOUR stages documented. Modifier-inversion STABLE at three consecutive degrees (18-20). Track fifth stage or continued modifier.
Loop pedal lineage: Frisell (1) to Watts solo (17). Paused at 18-19-20. Track resumption.
Capitol Hill geography: TWO entries (2, 18). Monge performs there but is Rainier Valley-based. Track.
Groove-as-composition: THREE entries (5, 18, 19). Stable. Monge's groove is guitar-based and solo. Track.
Band-as-collective: TWO entries (18 fixed, 19 collective). Stable.
Multi-degree artist: ONE instance (Watts 17/18). Track.
Neo-soul thread: TWO entries (18, 19). Stable. Monge is soul-adjacent but Americana Soul, not neo-soul proper.
Collective-as-organizational-type: ONE entry (19). Track.
Syncopation thread: ONE entry (19). Monge uses syncopation in her guitar playing but not as primary technique. Track.
Eastern Washington origin: TWO entries (Lanphere 12 Wenatchee, Monge 20 Spokane). NEW thread.
Self-taught thread: TWO entries (Berry 5, Monge 20). Extended.
Women-departure thread: THREE entries (Anderson 3, Bown 6, Monge 20). All three left Seattle. NEW thread.
Studio Litho / Seattle recording ecosystem: ONE entry (Monge 20 via Stone Gossard connection). NEW thread.
Busking / street performance: ONE entry (Monge 20). NEW thread.
Duwamish acknowledgment: RESOLVED. Retroactive to all degrees.

key_albums.title: Steadfast
key_albums.label: Self-released
key_albums.significance: First studio release. Produced by Grammy winner Pete Stewart. Released at SIFF Cinema Uptown screening of Find Your Way: A Busker's Documentary. First recordings with a band beyond solo voice and guitar.

key_albums.title: Stone
key_albums.label: Self-released
key_albums.significance: Self-produced. Recorded at Studio Litho by Stone Gossard (Pearl Jam). Engineered by Rachel Field. Record release show: sold-out Triple Door on Monge's 30th birthday (January 16, 2017) -- three blocks from Pike Place Market. Connection to Seattle recording ecosystem through Studio Litho.

key_albums.title: Carry On
key_albums.label: Self-released
key_albums.significance: Self-produced. Recorded at Robert Lang Studios and Studio Litho. KEXP premiere of lead single 'Be Mine'. Release party at Dimitriou's Jazz Alley with Naomi Wachira (November 2018). Robert Lang Studios connection (Nirvana recorded parts of In Utero there).

key_albums.title: Whitney Monge Live with The Seattle Symphony
key_albums.label: Self-released
key_albums.significance: The career's defining document. Twelve original songs orchestrated by Andrew Joslyn, performed by the Grammy-winning Seattle Symphony at Benaroya Hall (October 2, 2020). Conducted by Lee Mills with forty-piece orchestra. The orchestral arrangements reveal jazz-informed harmonic structure beneath folk-soul surface. Released August 27, 2021. Select songs on limited-edition vinyl.

degree_neighbors.name: Ernestine Anderson
degree_neighbors.relationship: First and third women in the engine. Both Black women. Both vocal-primary. Anderson: Central District, left and returned. Monge: Rainier Valley, built career then left. Mirror images of the departure pattern. Fourteen-degree gender gap (7-19) now broken.

degree_neighbors.name: Patti Bown
degree_neighbors.relationship: Second and third women. Bown: pianist-composer who left Seattle for New York (The Erasure). Monge: singer-songwriter-guitarist who left for Asheville/Nashville. All three women in the engine eventually left. Different instruments, different eras, same departure thread.

degree_neighbors.name: Bill Frisell
degree_neighbors.relationship: Guitar thread bookends. Frisell (degree 1): guitar-as-texture with effects and looping. Monge (degree 20): guitar-as-rhythm-section, acoustic, percussive, raw. Opposite poles of guitar practice across nineteen degrees.

degree_neighbors.name: Maktub
degree_neighbors.relationship: Neo-soul thread and jazz-as-modifier pattern. Three consecutive jazz-as-modifier degrees (18-20). Organizational arc: band (18) to collective (19) to solo (20) -- full circle back to solo artist.

degree_neighbors.name: Fly Moon Royale
degree_neighbors.relationship: Immediate predecessor. Collective (19) to solo (20). Organizational extremes: rotating-multiple-musicians vs. single-artist-solo-acoustic. The return to solo is in a transformed state (solo-with-orchestral-collaboration, not solo-as-default).

degree_neighbors.name: Overton Berry
degree_neighbors.relationship: Self-taught thread. Berry (degree 5): autodidact, no formal education. Monge (degree 20): quit Art Institute, self-trained via busking. Both Black artists. Both developed art through sustained public performance.

degree_neighbors.name: Don Lanphere
degree_neighbors.relationship: Eastern Washington thread. Lanphere (degree 12): Wenatchee. Monge (degree 20): Spokane. First two artists from east of the Cascades. Both came west to Seattle.

garfield_thread.degree_0: Quincy Jones -- attended late 1940s, class of 1950. Arranger/producer. Left Seattle.
garfield_thread.degree_3: Ernestine Anderson -- attended late 1940s, same era as Jones. Vocalist. Left and returned.
garfield_thread.degree_4: Floyd Standifer -- Central District post-WWII, educational path not confirmed as Garfield. Multi-instrumentalist and educator. Never left.
garfield_thread.degree_5: Overton Berry -- Central District native, self-taught. No institutional music education. Autodidact path.
garfield_thread.degree_6: Patti Bown -- Central District native, Seattle University (not Garfield). Formal training via Jesuit university.
garfield_thread.degree_7: Julian Priester -- NOT a Garfield product. DuSable High School (Chicago) under Captain Dyett. Cornish College of the Arts faculty.
garfield_thread.degree_8: Gary Peacock -- NOT a Garfield product. Burley, Idaho. Westlake College (LA). U.S. Army (Germany).
garfield_thread.degree_9: Hadley Caliman -- NOT a Garfield product. Idabel, Oklahoma. Los Angeles jazz scene. Cornish College of the Arts faculty.
garfield_thread.degree_10: Bud Shank -- NOT a Garfield product. Dayton, Ohio. University of North Carolina. Stan Kenton Orchestra. Los Angeles / Pacific Jazz.
garfield_thread.degree_11: Joe Brazil -- NOT a Garfield product. Detroit, Michigan. Detroit jazz scene. Settled in Central District as salon-model educator.
garfield_thread.degree_12: Don Lanphere -- NOT a Garfield product. Wenatchee, Washington. Northwestern University. NYC 52nd Street bebop scene.
garfield_thread.degree_13: Marc Seales -- NOT confirmed as Garfield product. Seattle native. University of Washington (DMA).
garfield_thread.degree_14: Aaron Parks -- NOT confirmed as Garfield product. Born Seattle 1983. UW pre-college at 14, MSM at 16.
garfield_thread.degree_15: Thomas Marriott -- NOT confirmed as Garfield product. Born Seattle 1979. UW jazz studies under Seales.
garfield_thread.degree_16: Jeff Lorber -- NOT a Garfield product. Born Philadelphia, PA. Berklee College of Music. Portland, OR then Seattle area.
garfield_thread.degree_17: Reggie Watts -- NOT a Garfield product. Born Stuttgart, Germany. Great Falls, Montana. Cornish College of the Arts.
garfield_thread.degree_18: Maktub -- NOT a Garfield product. Band formed on Capitol Hill, Seattle.
garfield_thread.degree_19: Fly Moon Royale -- NOT a Garfield product. Collective formed in Central District, Seattle.
garfield_thread.degree_20: Whitney Monge -- NOT a Garfield product. From Spokane, WA. Art Institute of Seattle (enrolled, quit after two quarters). Self-trained via eight years of busking at Pike Place Market. Fourteenth consecutive non-Garfield degree. First artist from eastern Washington.

cultural_patterns.institution_type: the street laureate -- non-institutional origin (quit Art Institute after two quarters), built career through public-space performance at Pike Place Market (eight years), reached the city's highest artistic institutions (Seattle Symphony at Benaroya Hall), eventually departed; the street is both the training ground and the credential
cultural_patterns.gender: female (Black woman; third woman in the engine; broke fourteen-degree gender gap 7-19; performed with Black Opry at Newport Folk Festival 2023; passionate about working with youth, women, and people of color)
cultural_patterns.gender_barrier: BROKEN -- first woman since Patti Bown degree 6; fourteen-degree streak of male/male-led artists (7-19) ends; distribution shifts from 2:18 to 3:18 (women:men); all three women in the engine are Black; all three eventually left Seattle

duwamish_acknowledgment: The Seattle 360 engine documents music made on Duwamish land. Every artist (degrees 0-20) created their music on the ancestral territory of the Duwamish Tribal Organization, the Muckleshoot Indian Tribe, and related Coast Salish peoples. Rainier Valley sits along the Duwamish River watershed. The city takes its name from Chief Si'ahl (Seattle), Treaty of Point Elliott 1855. The Duwamish Tribe remains federally unrecognized. This acknowledgment applies retroactively to all twenty-one releases and is inherited by every subsequent release.
